entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 4
entries.excerpt: Having spoken of Adam’s expulsion from the garden of Eden, of the Cherubim, and of the edge of the sword that guarded paradise, Moses turns to recount the birth of Cain and Abel, the offering of their sacrifices, and says: “And Adam knew Eve… and she bore Cain and said, I have gotten a man” (Gen 4:1) — not from Adam who had known her, but from the Lord who had formed the fruit in her womb. “And she again bore… Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground” (Gen 4:2) — that is, when they had come of age, the one became a shepherd and the other a tiller.…
